title: Add supports_ephemeral field to AppEvent::SessionReady
description:
## Why

AppEvent::SessionReady currently carries only api_url and account_id. The UI has no way to know whether ephemeral events will ever be delivered. This results in a typing indicator area that is permanently empty on servers that do not support the feature, with no explanation.

## What to do

In crates/jmap-chat-egui/src/event.rs, add the field:

```rust
AppEvent::SessionReady {
    api_url: String,
    account_id: String,
    supports_ephemeral: bool,  // true iff typing indicators and presence will be delivered
},
```

In client_task.rs, derive the value from ws_url (which is computed by the tracing bead):

```rust
send_event(&tx, &ctx, AppEvent::SessionReady {
    api_url: ...,
    account_id: ...,
    supports_ephemeral: ws_url.is_some(),
});
```

Update the match arm in app.rs that handles AppEvent::SessionReady to accept the new field (store it in AppState — see sibling bead).

## Acceptance criteria

- Compiles with no warnings
- AppEvent::SessionReady carries the field
- client_task.rs sets it from ws_url.is_some()
close_reason: supports_ephemeral field added to AppEvent::SessionReady; set from ws_url.is_some()
